content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 2, 1989

Patrick Carroll Shea
Lillick & McHose
101 West Broadway, 18th Floor
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-105

Dear Mr. Shea:

You have requested advice concerning the duties of San Diego
County Supervisor Susan Golding under the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ This letter confirms that
your letter dated February 9, 1989, accurately summarizes the
telephone advice I provided to you on that date.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any further
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

LILLICK & McHOSE
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
101 WEST BROADWAY, 18TH FLOOR
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
TELEPHONE (619) 234-5000

725 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIF. 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 488-7100

IRA S. LILLICK (1875-1967)

CABLES "LILLICKMCHOSE"
INTERNATIONAL TELEX 695785
TELECOPIER (619) 236-1905

February 13, 1989

DIRECT DIAL:
(619) 544-3177

Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Citizens for Susan Golding
1984 Campaign Deficiencies/Funds on Deposit From
1988 Campaign
Effect of Proposition 73
Our File No. GOL12-001

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Thank you for speaking with me last week regarding the
above matter.

Enclosed you will find a copy of my letter to my client,
San Diego County Supervisor Susan Golding regarding same.

At your convenience, I would appreciate receiving from you
confirmation of this advice.

The purpose of this correspondence is merely to avoid any
controversy and possible embarrassment to Ms. Golding in a
subsequent campaign as a result of the actions intended to be
taken respecting use of the 1988 surplus for the 1984 deficit.

Please feel free to call me with any questions you have
regarding the above.

Sincerely,

LILLICK & McHOSE

By: Patrick Carroll Shea
    Professional Corporation

By: ____________________________
    Patrick Carroll Shea

PCS:bc
dictated but not read

LILLICK & McHOSE
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

IRA B. LILLICK (1878-1967)

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

725 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET

CABLES "LILLICK&MCHOSE"
INTERNATIONAL TELEX 993755
TELECOPIER (619) 235-1995

101 WEST BROADWAY, 18TH FLOOR
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
TELEPHONE (619) 234-5000

LOS ANGELES, CALIF 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 488-7100

February 9, 1989

DIRECT DIAL NO.

Honorable Susan Golding
San Diego County Board of Supervisors
1600 Pacific Coast Highway
Room 335
San Diego, California 92101

CONFIDENTIAL

Re: Citizens for Susan Golding
1984 Campaign Deficiencies/Funds on Deposit from
1988 Campaign
Effect of Proposition 73
Our File No. GOL12-001

Dear Susan:

You have asked me to advise you regarding the ability
of your campaign committee, Citizens for Susan Golding, to use
contributions raised in the successful primary election of 1988
to pay off the deficit of the 1984 campaign. This question has
surfaced as a result of the enactment of last year's
Proposition 73 which restricts the use of campaign
contributions to the specific campaign identified to the
contributor at the time the contribution is made.

The enactment of Proposition 73 provided for an
effective date of January 1, 1989. From and after that date,
contributions made to an individual running for a particular
office may only be used by that individual in connection with
the campaign for the designated office and may not be
transferred to other candidates, nor used for campaigns other
than that announced at the time the contribution was made. You
have a surplus of campaign contributions from your successful
1988 primary campaign victory and you would like to use those
funds to pay off the debt incurred by your committee in
connection with the 1984 campaign. Since this transfer would
occur in February of 1989, you are concerned about the effect
of Proposition 73 on the transfer.

The Fair Political Practices Commission, through
Attorney Kathryn E. Donovan, has confirmed that the effective
date of Proposition 73 restricts only the use of those
contributions received on and after January 1, 1989.
Therefore, according to the FPPC, you may use the 1988 surplus
to pay off the 1984 deficit, provided the funds in the surplus
account were solicited and received in accordance with then
applicable County ordinances respecting campaign contributions
to persons running for a supervisor's office.

Honorable Susan Golding
February 9, 1989
Page Two

CONFIDENTIAL

As always, I send my best wishes.

Sincerely,

LILLICK & McHOSE

PATRICK CARROLL SHEA
A Professional Corporation


By: _______________________
     Patrick Carroll Shea

PCS:wl
